zh.Aston by Sutton, Cheshire, England: 英國爾阿斯頓 白 塞頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Aston II於大約1336年出生在英國爾阿斯頓 白 塞頓時，他的父親Robert de Aston43歲，母親Felice Hawarden40歲。他於1366年與Fillote de Hawarden在英國越可爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1385年1月9日在英國肯特多福 卡素享年50歲，埋葬在英國肯特多福。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert de Aston II于大约1336年出生在英国爾阿斯頓 白 塞頓时，他的父亲Robert de Aston43岁，母亲Felice Hawarden40岁。他于1366年与Fillote de Hawarden在英国越可爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1385年1月9日在英国肯特多福 卡素享年50岁，埋葬在英国肯特多福。

nameMeanings.name: Aston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: from the Middle English personal name Ad(e)stan, which is usually from Old English Æthelstān (see Alston ). It may also be from the much rarer Old English name Ēadstān.
habitational name from any of several places in England called Aston. Most were named with Old English ēast ‘east’ + tūn ‘settlement’. In a few cases the first element is æsc ‘ash tree’.

variants.name: Alston
meanings: from the Middle English personal name Alstan, which is a coalescence of several different Old English personal names: Æthelstān ‘noble stone’, Ælfstān ‘elf stone’, Ealdstān ‘old stone’, or Ealhstān ‘temple stone’.

habitational name from any of various places called Alston (in Cumbria, Lancashire, Devon, and Somerset) or Alstone (in Gloucestershire and Staffordshire). With the exception of Alston in Cumbria, which is formed with the Old Norse personal name Halfdan, these placenames all consist of an Old English personal name + Old English tūn ‘settlement’, for example Ælfsige in the case of Alstone in Gloucestershire.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Aston II was born about 1336, in Aston by Sutton, Cheshire, England, his father, Robert de Aston, was 43 and his mother, Felice Hawarden, was 40. He married Fillote de Hawarden in 1366, in Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 9 January 1385, in Dover Castle, Kent, England, at the age of 50, and was buried in Dover, Kent, England.

fr.Aston, Cheshire, England: Aston by Sutton, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Aston II est né environ 1336, à Aston by Sutton, Cheshire, Angleterre, son père, Robert de Aston, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Felice Hawarden, avait 40 ans. Il a épousé Fillote de Hawarden en 1366, à Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 9 janvier 1385, à Dover Castle, Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 50 ans, et a été enterré à Dover, Kent, Angleterre.

de.Aston, Cheshire, England: Aston by Sutton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Robert de Aston II um 1336 in Aston by Sutton, Cheshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert de Aston 43 und seine Mutter Felice Hawarden 40. Er hat 1366 in Yorkshire, England Fillote de Hawarden geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 9. Januar 1385 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Dover Castle, Kent, England und wurde in Dover, Kent, England begraben.

Lui sposato Fillote de Hawarden nel 1366, nel Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì il 9 gennaio 1385, nel Dover Castle, Kent, England, all'età di 50 anni, e fu sepolto a Dover, Kent, England.

ko.Aston, Cheshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 애스턴 비 수턴
ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Aston II(이)가 대략 1336년 영국 체실르 애스턴 비 수턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Aston(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Felice Hawarden(은)는 40세였다. 그는 1366년 영국 요르크실르에서 Fillote de Hawarden(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1385년 1월 9일 영국 켄트 도버 카스틀에서 50세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 켄트 도버에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Aston II nasceu aproximadamente 1336, em Aston by Sutton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert de Aston, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Felice Hawarden, tinha 40 anos. Ele casou-se com Fillote de Hawarden em 1366, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 9 de janeiro de 1385, em Dover Castle, Kent, Inglaterra, com 50 anos, e foi sepultado em Dover, Kent, Inglaterra.

ru.Aston, Cheshire, England: астон би суттон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Aston II родился около 1336 г. в местности астон би суттон, чешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert de Aston было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Felice Hawarden, 40. Он женился на женщине по имени Fillote de Hawarden в 1366 г. в местности еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер 9 января 1385 г. в местности довир кастл, кэнт, Англия в возрасте 50 лет и был похоронен в местности довир, кэнт, Англия.

Se casó con Fillote de Hawarden en 1366, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija.
